axioms.name: Causal or normative affirmed
axioms.description: For any non-neutral primitive, some admissible framework affirms it. Companion to framework_relativity: together they establish that causal and normative primitives are genuinely framework-variant. Some frameworks affirm them, some deny them. Required for framework_contestability_lemma; without it FrameworkVariant cannot be instantiated for any concrete primitive.

axioms.name: Framework relativity
axioms.description: For any non-neutral primitive, some admissible framework denies it. Encodes that causal and normative conclusions are inherently contested across interpretive frameworks. Sufficient for the lower bound (not_neutral_if_causal_or_normative) alone. Fails in domains where causal or normative consensus exists.

axioms.name: Neutral primitives undisputed
axioms.description: Neutral primitives are not denied by any admissible framework. Pure existence and identity claims are framework-invariant. Required for the upper bound (neutral_if_only_neutral); the biconditional holds only when both this axiom and framework_relativity are satisfied. Fails in domains where identity conditions are themselves contested.
